assertions.description: Initial state shows a target function (square wave) with n=1 Fourier term approximation — the approximation is visibly poor, posing the problem
Dragging the purple slider point right adds more Fourier basis terms
Dragging the purple slider point left removes Fourier basis terms
More basis terms → better approximation → lower error, demonstrating how infinite dimensions allow arbitrarily close approximation
Student sees that adding more orthogonal basis functions continues to improve the fit indefinitely — infinite dimensions means no cap on approximation quality
assertions.verification: With n=15+ terms, the purple curve closely matches the orange square wave target
